ops.title: `ab` is the ONE agent-browser entry point — routes Browserbase vs local by env var; never hand-roll raw agent-browser calls in the abwc skill
ops.body:
`ab` (`bin/ab` in the agent-browser-with-cookies plugin) is the **universal** wrapper for every agent-browser call in that skill. It routes to Browserbase when `BROWSERBASE_API_KEY` (or the 1Password fallback) resolves, else to the local default (Clark); `--local` forces local. The skill must drive EVERY call through `"$ab"` — open, seed, verify, task, cleanup.

**Why:** the user was emphatic ("it should ALWAYS use ab, which routes to local default or browserbase based on the env var — we've been over this already"). skill 0.10.0 had drifted: it documented `ab` as Browserbase-only and told agents to hand-roll a raw `agent-browser --session abwc` + FIFO recipe for local mode. A getaway gatherer followed that prose literally and wedged the daemon. Do NOT reintroduce raw `agent-browser` invocations into the skill prose — local recovery paths included (use `"$ab" --local …`).

**How to apply:** abwc 0.11.0 (cc-skills 7d640c9) made this real — `ab mode` prints the resolved backend, `bin/abwc-seed` resolves the backend ONCE at entry and pins every child call to it (local → `--local` on every call; browserbase → `export BROWSERBASE_API_KEY` so no child re-resolves). When editing this skill: one `ab` path, mode resolved once, no per-call backend re-resolution that could split a flow across two backends. See [[cookiesync-web-storage]] for the 0.11.0 seeding-helper details and [[agent-browser-state-handoff]] for the FIFO mechanics.
